channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump: My presidential win led to Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump lauded the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal struck on Wednesday, saying it would not have happened without his victory in November over Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Iran's president denies claim that Iran tried to assassinate Trump
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian denied claims that his country ever explored an assassination attempt against President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel supplied Iran with centrifuge platforms containing explosives, top official acknowledges
item.description: Israel supplied Iran with centrifuge platforms containing explosives for its nuclear enrichment program in an act of sabotage.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russian missile attack forces Ukraine to shut down power grid
item.description: Ukrainian officials say Russia has launched a major ballistic and cruise missile attack on regions across Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova And Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangladesh Supreme Court acquits ex-Prime Minister Zia, clearing the way for her to run in elections
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s Supreme Court on Wednesday acquitted former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia in the last corruption case against her, paving the way for her to run in elections that an interim government says will be held either in December or in the first half of 2026.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's prime minister demands Russia explain what happened to Australian POW
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said on Wednesday his government will take the “strongest action possible” if Russia has harmed an Australian who was taken prisoner while fighting for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia's top diplomat praises Trump's views on Ukraine conflict
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s top diplomat said Tuesday that Moscow is open for talks with President-elect Donald Trump and praised him for pointing to NATO's plan to embrace Ukraine as a root cause of the nearly 3-year-old conflict.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly half worldwide hold antisemitic views: ADL
item.description: Nearly half of people worldwide hold antisemitic views, according to a new survey by the Anti-Defamation League (ADL).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kate returns to cancer center where she was treated to offer gratitude to medical teams
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales visited the hospital where she was treated for cancer on Tuesday, offering gratitude to the medical teams who helped her and give support to patients now undergoing treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polish town invites Musk to buy castle for his European HQ
item.description: Tech billionaire is reportedly looking for a sizeable location, possibly a castle in Italy, as a hub for his operations in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill 18 in Gaza, medics say, as Hamas accepts a ceasefire draft
item.description: Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip killed at least 18 people overnight including women and children, health officials said Tuesday, as Israel and Hamas appeared to be narrowing in on a ceasefire deal to end the 15-month war and release dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korean soldiers in Russia resort to suicide amid capture of first POWs by Ukraine
item.description: North Korean soldiers fighting in Russia's Kursk region against Ukraine have begun resorting to drastic measures to avoid capture, including "finishing off" fellow soldiers and suicide by grenade.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After economic meltdown and war with Israel, Lebanon's new prime minister vows to rebuild
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s prime minister-designate vowed Tuesday to work on building a modern state in the crisis-hit country, saying his priorities will be to rebuild the destruction caused by a yearlong war with Israel and work on pulling the small nation out of its historic economic meltdown.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas has accepted draft agreement for Gaza cease-fire, hostage release: officials
item.description: Hamas has reportedly agreed to a draft of a multi-phase cease-fire deal with Israel that would include the release of hostages who were taken into Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malaysia is home to the world's largest hotel, with over 7K rooms for guests
item.description: The Guinness World Record for the largest hotel by number of rooms goes to First World Hotel in Malaysia. The hotel boasts over 7,000 hotel rooms for guests to lounge in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel and Hamas getting closer amid concerns terror group rearming in Gaza
item.description: Israel and Hamas are nearing a cease-fire deal amid escalating death toll; 5 IDF soldiers were killed in Gaza on Monday amid growing warnings that Hamas is rearming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Tsunami alert lifted after magnitude 6.6 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan issued a tsunami advisory on Monday after a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck the southwest of the country. The public warnings to stay away from coastal areas were later lifted. The agency initially gave the magnitude estimate at 6.9, but later revised it down.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden calls for immediate cease-fire in call with Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a phone call on Sunday to discuss the ongoing ceasefire and hostage release negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, told the pope Saturday he was a recipient of the medal.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Russia says it will continue oil and gas projects despite US sanctions
item.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has denounced U.S. sanctions against Moscow's energy sector and said the country would carry on with large oil and gas projects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation
